feed.title: Mayur Sinha
feed.subtitle: Security engineering leader writing about AI security, Web3 security, cloud-native security, and engineering systems.

entry.title: MCP Visor - Runtime policy enforcement for AI agents
entry.summary: MCP Visor turns MCP tool execution into a deterministic policy boundary: inspect the tool call, enforce the rule, redact secrets, require approval, and log the decision before the action reaches the server.

entry.title: Why grinding is a self-inflicted DDoS
entry.summary: Grinding inside an extractive system does not change the payoff matrix. It only increases throughput for someone else's infrastructure.

entry.title: How to eliminate an entire class of API authorization bugs
entry.summary: BOLA is not solved by asking every developer to remember object-level checks. It is solved by making authorization a reusable platform capability.

entry.title: MCP security - The new supply chain risk for AI agents
entry.summary: MCP servers are not harmless plugins. They are executable trust boundaries that shape what AI agents see, believe, and do.

entry.title: Epistemic security - The missing control plane for AI agents
entry.summary: AI agents do not only need runtime security. They need epistemic security: controls for provenance, uncertainty, evidence, memory, and human verification.

entry.title: The AI agent dilemma - Why we are outsourcing our reality
entry.summary: AI agents may not just change how we work. They may weaken our ability to know, verify, and justify what we believe.

entry.title: Game theory for cybersecurity: from reactive defense to strategic resilience
entry.summary: A practical guide to using game theory in cybersecurity, from Nash and Stackelberg models to APT defense, forensic readiness, and LLM-driven simulations.

entry.title: Threat Modeling Autonomous AI Agents in Production
entry.summary: A practical framework for threat modeling autonomous AI agents in production, with concrete attack paths, controls, and measurable security SLOs.

entry.title: Traditional manual code review is no longer sustainable
entry.summary: Code review is a historical approval gate that simply no longer matches the shape of modern work. Here is why we need to pivot.

entry.title: Why Transformer LLMs are better at finding code vulnerabilities than classical neural networks
entry.summary: A technical comparison of classical ANN pipelines and transformer LLMs for vulnerability detection.

entry.title: Vibe Engineering
entry.summary: Vibe Engineering

entry.title: Designing a living ecosystem of AI agents
entry.summary: AI agents ecosystem

entry.title: Life is cruel
entry.summary: Life is cruel

entry.title: Revisiting the Unabomber
entry.summary: Revisiting the Unabomber

entry.title: Exploring Philosophical Parallels - Nietzsche and Vedanta
entry.summary: Exploring Philosophical Parallels - Nietzsche and Vedanta

entry.title: Resolving the Epicurean Paradox - A Perspective from Advaita Vedanta
entry.summary: Resolving the Epicurean Paradox

entry.title: Elon Musk on xAI
entry.summary: Elon Musk on xAI

entry.title: Man is a machine
entry.summary: Man is a machine

entry.title: Was Thanos Right?
entry.summary: Was Thanos Right?

entry.title: Reframing Application Security
entry.summary: Application security should not be a late-stage interruption. The mature AppSec move is to improve developer feedback loops, reduce cognitive load, and build secure defaults into the engineering system.
